questions.q: By what affectionate title was Jagjivan Ram widely known in Indian politics?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram was popularly addressed as 'Babuji' throughout his long political career.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram is noted as the longest-serving member of which body?
questions.explanation: He is recognised as the longest-serving member of the Indian Cabinet.

questions.q: Which of these senior posts did Jagjivan Ram hold?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram served as Deputy Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Jagjivan Ram born?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram was born in 1908 in the Bihar region of British India.

questions.q: Which key defence-related cabinet portfolio did Jagjivan Ram hold?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram served as India's Defence Minister during his career.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram's career is primarily associated with which branch of government?
questions.explanation: He was a long-serving cabinet minister and parliamentarian, part of the executive and legislature.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram held cabinet portfolios over a span of roughly how long?
questions.explanation: He held various cabinet portfolios across several decades of public life.

questions.q: Which house of Parliament did Jagjivan Ram represent as a directly elected member?
questions.explanation: He was repeatedly elected to the Lok Sabha, the directly elected lower house.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram was a prominent voice for which broad cause in Indian public life?
options: Social justice and uplift of weaker sections
questions.explanation: He was a leading advocate for the social and political advancement of weaker sections.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram first became a Union minister at the time of which event?
questions.explanation: He joined the first government of independent India formed in 1947.

questions.q: In which year was Jagjivan Ram born?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram was born on 5 April 1908.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram became Defence Minister of India in which year, during the period of war that year?
questions.explanation: He was appointed Defence Minister in 1970 and held the post during the 1971 conflict.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram was a member of India's first cabinet under which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served in the first Union cabinet headed by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: Which portfolio did Jagjivan Ram notably hold in the years before independence-era cabinet, relating to transport infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Among his many portfolios, Jagjivan Ram served as Minister of Railways.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram became Deputy Prime Minister during which government?
options: The Janata Party government formed in 1977
questions.explanation: He served as Deputy Prime Minister in the Janata Party government formed after the 1977 election.

questions.q: In which year did Jagjivan Ram pass away?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram died on 6 July 1986.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram held the portfolio of Minister of Agriculture and Irrigation during which government?
options: The Indira Gandhi government in the 1970s
questions.explanation: He served as Minister of Agriculture and Irrigation in the Indira Gandhi government during the 1970s.

questions.q: From which kind of constituency was Jagjivan Ram repeatedly elected to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He was repeatedly elected to the Lok Sabha from a constituency in his home state of Bihar.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram was associated for most of his career with which political party?
questions.explanation: For most of his career he was a member of the Indian National Congress before joining the Janata fold in the late 1970s.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram is sometimes referred to as a 'permanent' figure in the cabinet because he was a minister under how many Prime Ministers?
questions.explanation: He served as a Union minister under several different Prime Ministers across decades.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram's daughter, Meira Kumar, later held which constitutional office?
questions.explanation: His daughter Meira Kumar served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha, the first woman to hold that office.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram entered legislative politics first through which body during the colonial period?
questions.explanation: He began his legislative career in a provincial legislature before independence.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram held the office of Labour Minister in the interim and first national governments around which year?
questions.explanation: He served as Labour Minister in the interim government and the first cabinet of independent India around 1946-1947.

questions.q: In which year did Jagjivan Ram leave the Congress to help form a new party shortly before the 1977 election?
questions.explanation: In early 1977 he left the Congress and founded the Congress for Democracy, which merged into the Janata fold.

questions.q: The party Jagjivan Ram founded in 1977 was named:
questions.explanation: He founded the Congress for Democracy in 1977, which soon merged with the Janata Party.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram was Defence Minister of India during the Indo-Pakistan war of which year?
questions.explanation: He held the Defence portfolio during the 1971 Indo-Pakistan war.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram earned a degree before entering politics from which university?
questions.explanation: He studied at and graduated from the University of Calcutta.

questions.q: In which decade did Jagjivan Ram serve continuously as a Union cabinet minister for the longest stretch?
options: From the late 1940s onward across multiple decades
questions.explanation: He served as a Union minister almost continuously from the late 1940s through the 1970s.

questions.q: Jagjivan Ram also served, for a period, as President of which national party organisation?
options: A unit of the Indian National Congress
questions.explanation: He held organisational leadership within the Indian National Congress during his long association with the party.

questions.q: The total span over which Jagjivan Ram held Union cabinet rank is best described as approximately:
questions.explanation: His cumulative service in the Union Cabinet stretched to roughly three decades, the longest on record.
